message.content:
Excellent! Finally have a prompt. The old finetune.py was still running. Let me kill it and try a cleaner approach.

{"analysis":"Got shell prompt back. The old finetune.py process was suspended. Need to kill it and try a better approach. My best accuracy was 0.603 with autotune. Let me try autotune on full data with no char n-grams (minn=0, maxn=0) to avoid the slowness issue.","plan":"1. Kill the suspended process. 2. Run autotune on full data for 5 minutes with minn=0, maxn=0 explicitly. 3. Quantize to fit under 150MB. 4. Save as /app/model.bin.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"kill -9 %1 2>/dev/null; echo 'KILLED'
","duration":2.0},{"keystrokes":"cat > /app/autotune.py << 'PYEOF'
import fasttext
import os

print('Running autotune (5min, no char ngrams)...', flush=True)
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train_full.txt',
    autotuneValidationFile='/app/test.txt',
    autotuneDuration=300,
    thread=4,
    verbose=2,
    minn=0,
    maxn=0
)

print('\nEvaluating...', flush=True)
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
print(f'Acc: {p:.4f}', flush=True)

model.save_model('/app/model_best.bin')
sz = os.path.getsize('/app/model_best.bin') / (1024*1024)
print(f'Size: {sz:.1f} MB', flush=True)

if sz > 150:
    print('Quantizing...', flush=True)
    model.quantize(input='/app/train_full.txt', retrain=True, epoch=10, lr=0.25, thread=4, dsub=2, qnorm=True)
    model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
    sz2 = os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') / (1024*1024)
    n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
    print(f'Quant: sz={sz2:.1f}MB acc={p:.4f}', flush=True)
else:
    os.rename('/app/model_best.bin', '/app/model.bin')
    print(f'Saved /app/model.bin ({sz:.1f} MB)', flush=True)

print('DONE', flush=True)
PYEOF
","duration":0.5},{"keystrokes":"PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 python3 /app/autotune.py
","duration":600.0}]}
